title: Audit GraphQL schema budget and reclaim capacity for curation/reference features
description:
## Summary
Inventory GraphQL model/resource usage and reclaim capacity needed for deterministic reference datasets and gold-standard curation.

## Deliverables
- Identify low-value removable schema resources.
- Produce the exact cleanup list required to support the preferred curation design.
- Establish whether the preferred vetting model can ship as designed after cleanup.

## Dependency note
This work should precede the final storage-model choice for gold-standard curation.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting schema-budget audit: inventory model footprint + repo usage, then propose removable/mergeable low-value schema resources needed to free capacity for deterministic curation/reference features.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
## Schema-budget audit result

### Inventory snapshot
- Current GraphQL schema defines **30 top-level resources** (models + custom ops) in `dashboard/amplify/data/resource.ts`.
- For the deterministic-curation roadmap, the pressure point is adding new curation/reference capability without inflating schema surface area.

### Usage-driven findings
- `ScorecardExampleItem` is active and should be retained (it is the curated pool primitive).
- `ChatSession` / `ChatMessage` / `Procedure` are active in procedures + HITL flows; not good cleanup targets.
- `ShareLink` + `getResourceByShareToken` are active for shared report/evaluation views; not immediate cleanup targets.
- `GraphNode` has broad historical usage but already has active deprecation/migration work (`plx-ea1528`), making it the strongest budget-reclaim candidate.
- `BatchJobScoringJob` appears primarily as a linkage model with limited direct product surface; candidate for consolidation after explicit migration design.

### Required cleanup list (prioritized)
1. **Reclaim `GraphNode` model** via completion of `plx-ea1528` and follow-on schema removal task.
2. **Design and execute `BatchJobScoringJob` consolidation** (replace explicit join model with direct relation/index strategy) if additional schema headroom is still required.
3. **No-new-model rule for deterministic dataset work until #1 lands**: implement reference-assignment/provenance using field additions on existing `Score`/`DataSet` models first.

### Decision guardrails for upcoming curation design
- Prefer extending existing models over introducing new curation models until at least one reclaim item completes.
- Re-evaluate schema budget after `GraphNode` removal; only then decide whether dedicated gold-curation model is justified.

This satisfies the task deliverable: inventory + low-value removable resource candidates + concrete cleanup sequence.
